thesis: Infleqtion is the first publicly traded neutral-atom quantum technology company, offering a unique pure-play on quantum computing and sensing. With $32.5M revenue (up 13% YoY), a $40M 2026 guidance, and $540M+ in SPAC proceeds, the company has both near-term revenue validation from quantum sensing products and long-term optionality on quantum computing commercialization. The NVIDIA partnership, DARPA/DoD contracts, and 235+ patents create a differentiated position in the emerging quantum ecosystem.

drivers: 100 logical qubits achieved by 2028 with major commercial computing contracts
Defense spending acceleration drives sensing revenue to $50M+ annually
Strategic acquisition by big tech (NVIDIA, Google, Microsoft) at significant premium
Quantum computing TAM materializes faster than expected ($100B+ by 2035)

risks: Revenue misses $40M guidance; quantum computing progress stalls
Cash burn accelerates requiring dilutive capital raise
Quantum winter reduces sector funding and valuations
Competing technologies (trapped ion, superconducting) achieve commercial scale first

base_case.assumptions: $40-60M revenue by 2027 with sensing stabilizing at $30M+; computing revenue begins scaling; path to EBITDA breakeven by 2028-2029; analyst coverage expands to 3-5 firms

catalysts.event: Q1 2026 earnings — first public earnings report

key_risks: Pre-profitability with significant cash burn; 3-5 year path to breakeven
Quantum computing commercialization timeline uncertain; history of over-promising
Revenue concentration in government contracts subject to budget cycles
Competitive technology risk from trapped ion, superconducting, or photonic approaches
SPAC merger overhang and limited institutional coverage create volatility
